description: Energy & Power Solution Group is Mitsubishi Corporation's energy business organization.
Diamond Global Energy/Diamond Gas International is a Mitsubishi LNG marketing and trading subsidiary in Singapore.
MC Energies oversees Mitsubishi's US energy business.
Mitsubishi participates in LNG Canada through Diamond LNG Canada Ltd.
Diamond LNG Canada invested in and participates in LNG Canada; it also procures feed gas and offtakes LNG.
Canadian upstream gas is transported roughly 670 km by pipeline to the west coast for liquefaction.
Pipeline delivers gas to the LNG Canada export terminal/liquefaction project.
Mitsubishi supplies LNG Canada offtake to Asian customers through Diamond Gas International.
DGI markets LNG to customers across Asia; Korea is a key Asian LNG demand market, though this edge is contextual rather than a named contract in the source.
Mitsubishi agreed to acquire Aethon/Haynesville shale gas assets for about USD 5.2bn.
Aethon gas has favorable access to multiple LNG export terminals including Cameron LNG.
Mitsubishi owns tolling capacity equivalent to one liquefaction train at Cameron LNG.
CIMA Energy is cited as part of Mitsubishi's North American midstream marketing and logistics platform.
Diamond Generating Corporation is part of Mitsubishi's North American power platform.
Mitsubishi says the Aethon investment can accelerate an integrated US chain from upstream gas to power generation, data center development, chemicals and related businesses.
PETRONAS and Mitsubishi have a 46-year LNG partnership.
Mitsubishi extended its 10% equity shareholding in MLNG Dua for the next decade.
Mitsubishi reinvested a 10% equity shareholding in MLNG Tiga.
MLNG Dua is one of the ventures in the PETRONAS LNG Complex.
MLNG Tiga is one of the ventures in the PETRONAS LNG Complex.
PETRONAS LNG Complex supplies diverse public/private customers in Japan; broader Asian industrial demand relevance is contextual.
Mitsubishi partnered with Shell and Brunei government support to establish Brunei LNG.
Brunei government supported and invested in Brunei LNG establishment.
Mitsubishi participated in Brunei LNG, building a 50-year value chain covering upstream, liquefaction, sales and transportation.
First LNG shipment from Brunei arrived in Japan in 1972 and stable supply continued.
Donggi-Senoro LNG is jointly owned by Mitsubishi, KOGAS, Pertamina and Medco; MC is operator/largest stake.
KOGAS is a joint owner of Donggi-Senoro LNG.
Pertamina is a joint owner of Donggi-Senoro LNG.
Medco is a joint owner of Donggi-Senoro LNG.
KIET reports Hormuz risk can raise Korean manufacturing costs through energy and linked industrial materials.
Oman is outside/adjacent to Hormuz and considered a geographic diversification option for Korea.
Korea's MOTIE discussed LNG and energy supply-chain cooperation with Oman LNG leadership.
Berkshire holds long-term stakes in five Japanese trading houses: ITOCHU, Marubeni, Mitsubishi, Mitsui and Sumitomo.
Mitsubishi Corporation is one of the five Japanese trading houses in Berkshire's Japanese investment basket.
AI/data center power demand is a growing driver that connects energy security to industrial competitiveness; Korea relevance is strategic inference.
Mitsubishi strategic investment in MidOcean Energy (2024.4); added ~6 indirect LNG projects.
KOGAS holds its Oman LNG participation through the KOREA LNG vehicle.
KOREA LNG holds a 5.0% equity stake in Oman LNG.
Mitsubishi holds 75% of the Sulawesi LNG Development holding vehicle.
KOGAS holds 25% of the Sulawesi LNG Development holding vehicle.
Sulawesi LNG Development (MC 75 / KOGAS 25) is the holding vehicle for Donggi-Senoro LNG.
